%% solve(Nums) -> Max fixed points
solve(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    
    % Initialize F: F[k] stores the max fixed points for a subsequence of length k.
    % Use -1 to represent unreachable states. F[0] = 0.
    % F is represented as a list where index k corresponds to F[k].
    F_init = lists:insert_at(0, 0, lists:replicate(N, -1)),

    % Iterate over each element nums[i] in the input array
    F_final = lists:foldl(fun(F, i) ->
        Nums_i = element(i, Nums),
        
        % F_new is the result of processing nums[i]. We iterate k backwards 
        % to ensure F[k] used is from the state before processing nums[i].
        F_updated = lists:foldl(fun(F_current, k) ->
            % Check if F[k] is reachable
            case lists:nth(k, F_current) of
                -1 -> F_current;
                FP_k ->
                    % nums[i] is appended to a subsequence of length k. New length k' = k + 1.
                    k_prime = k + 1,
                    
                    % Check if nums[i] matches the required position k
                    is_fixed_point = (Nums_i == k),
                    
                    new_fp_count = FP_k + (if is_fixed_point -> 1; true -> 0 end),
                    
                    % Get current max FP for length k'
                    current_fp_k_prime = case lists:nth(k_prime, F_current) of
                                            -1 -> -1;
                                            FP_k_prime -> FP_k_prime end,

                    New_FP_k_prime = max(current_fp_k_prime, new_fp_count),
                    
                    % Update F_current list

            end

    % The result is the maximum value in F_final
    lists:max(F_final).

% Helper function to replicate a value N times
replicate(N, Value) when N > 0 ->
    [Value | replicate(N - 1, Value)];
replicate(_, _) ->
    []
.
